title: Clarify WAI-Spoke (v3) vs WAI-Harness/spoke (v4) in ARCHITECTURE.md
author: documentation-advisor
perceive: ARCHITECTURE.md describes WAI-Spoke as the principal state directory, but the spoke has migrated to v4 (WAI-Harness/spoke). New engineers reading this doc will use outdated directory paths.
execute: 1. Read ARCHITECTURE.md section 2 (Directory Structure, lines 40-100) and identify all WAI-Spoke references
2. Update section 2 to note: 'WAI-Spoke (v3 legacy) has been superseded by WAI-Harness/spoke (v4); maintain only legacy sessions/reference data'
3. Add a migration callout: 'New work uses WAI-Harness/spoke structure; see WAI-State.json for harness status'
4. Ensure directory tree reflects v4 structure (WAI-Harness/spoke/local, /managed, /advisors)
verify: ARCHITECTURE.md clearly documents both v3 and v4 structures; readers understand which is current; no stale directory paths remain
acceptance_criteria: ARCHITECTURE.md section 2 identifies WAI-Spoke (v3) as legacy and WAI-Harness/spoke (v4) as current
Migration callout added directing readers to WAI-State.json for harness status
No stale WAI-Spoke paths remain in the document
